questions.text: When it comes to career growth and professional development — as a remote engineer building your personal brand, are you actively working on specific skills, certifications, or trying to position yourself for certain roles/industries? Or is it more of a 'see what interesting opportunities come up' thing?

When you're reading for fun — not learning or work-related — what genres or types of books actually pull you in? Are you into fiction (what kind?), non-fiction, memoirs, sci-fi, or something else entirely? Any authors or books that have stuck with you?

When you're in a new place or meet someone new, how do you actually go about building your local community — beyond volleyball? Do you seek out specific types of people, use apps like Meetup, just hang out at the same spots, or is it more organic than that?

When it comes to mindfulness or spiritual practices — do you have a daily thing you do (meditation, journaling, something else), or is it more like you pull from different tools depending on what you need in the moment? Any specific techniques or frameworks that actually work for you?

When you think about long-term financial security — aside from investing, what's your mindset about things like emergency savings, health insurance as a nomad, planning for potential healthcare needs as you age, or any other 'adulting' financial things that aren't just about growing wealth?
